etymology.description: semantic 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 黑(黑) (hēi) -> sound 'hēi' produced by mouth -> 'hey' as an interjection
etymology.interpretation: Combines 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth"), referring to speech or sound, with the phonetic 黑(黑) (hēi) to suggest the vocal sound 'hey'. The mouth indicates vocal utterance, while 黑 contributes the sound, leading to the character's use as an interjection 'hey'.

examples.chinese: 嘿(嘿)，你在做什么(你在做什麼)？
嘿(嘿)，能帮个忙吗(能幫個忙嗎)？
examples.pinyin: Hēi, néng bāng gè máng ma?
